_comment: Ground truth for the Gate V5 orientation fixture (ARCHITECTURE §7 V5). Authored and committed by the human maintainer; geometry generated parametrically and verified programmatically; all values computed from the committed file bytes (provenance rule, DECISIONS.md 2026-07-04). Agents assert against these values; they do not edit this file. Units: mm, mm^3.

hook.stl.what_it_is: A wall J-hook: back mounting plate (x in [0,6], y in [8,40]), half-annulus bottom curve (center (14,8), outer R 14, inner r 8, spanning y down to -6), upturned tip (x in [22,28], y in [8,20]); profile in the XY plane, extruded 8 mm in +Z. 24 segments per semicircle.
hook.stl.analytic_area_note: Profile polygon area 470.7535 mm^2 vs analytic 471.3451 mm^2 (0.13% chord deficit from 24-segment arcs) - inherent discretization, assert the MESH volume above, not the analytic one.

load_case.fixture_region: back-plate voxels adjacent to the x=0 plane (the wall face), full y/z extent of the plate (y in [8,40])
load_case.load_region: voxels at the inner surface of the upturned tip / inner curve near (x~22, y~8..14), where a hung object bears
load_case.note: Tags are set by coordinates in the test (STL has no face identity). The bending tension path runs through the curve (around the annulus) in the XY profile plane.

orientation_ground_truth.meaning: Print LYING FLAT: the flat XY face on the build plate, layers stacked through the 8 mm thickness. Layers are then parallel to the XY profile plane, so the bending tension path through the curve lies WITHIN layers (no interlayer tension), and the flat silhouette needs essentially zero support.

must_rank_below.meaning: Print UPRIGHT (as it hangs in use): layer planes are XZ, so the tension path through the curve crosses layer boundaries exactly at the stress concentration (the classic snapped-hook failure), AND the curve underside is a supported overhang.

orientation_ground_truth.assertion: ORDINAL ONLY: the M4.4 scorer must rank [0,0,1] strictly better than [0,1,0] for this part, material PLA (z_knockdown 0.55), under the load case above, and must rank [0,0,1] first among the full candidate set. Numeric score values are NOT pinned - the score function's scale is M4.4's to define; this fixture pins the ORDER. The [0,0,-1] direction is geometrically equivalent to [0,0,1] for this part (flat either face down) and tying with it is acceptable.
orientation_ground_truth.mechanism_requirement: Per §7 V5, the ranking must be driven at least in part by the z_knockdown penalty on tension across layer planes: with z_knockdown artificially set to 1.0 (isotropic), the stress-alignment term must lose its preference (the support term may still prefer flat) - a test SHOULD verify the penalty term responds to z_knockdown, not only the final ordering.
